old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-4b8","title":"S0-01: Tauri v2 + React + TypeScript + Vite project scaffold","description":"Create the initial project scaffold using Tauri v2 with a React + TypeScript + Vite frontend. Use 'npm create tauri-app@latest' or equivalent. The result should be a working Tauri app that opens a window with the default React starter page. Verify with 'npm run dev' (frontend only) and 'cargo tauri dev' (full app). Directory structure should follow Tauri v2 conventions: src/ for React frontend, src-tauri/ for Rust backend.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:28:24Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:28:24Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-qjq","title":"S0-02: Vitest setup with basic smoke test","description":"Install and configure Vitest as the test runner. Add vitest.config.ts with jsdom environment for DOM/SVG testing. Create a basic smoke test that verifies the app shell renders. Configure npm scripts: 'npm test' and 'npm run test:watch'. Vitest is chosen because it is Vite-native, fast, and TypeScript-first.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:28:30Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:28:30Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-3yn","title":"S0-03: Project structure — module registry, canvas component, app shell layout","description":"Establish the core project structure. Create: (1) App shell layout component with the standard layout: horizontal toolbar (top), layers panel (left, collapsible), canvas (center), properties panel (right, collapsible), status bar (bottom). (2) Module/tool registry system — a central registry where tools register themselves with a name, icon, and keyboard shortcut. (3) Placeholder components for each panel. Use Tailwind CSS for styling with a constrained palette (grays for chrome, white canvas, one accent color). No component library — all UI elements built from scratch.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:28:37Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:28:37Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-8hh","title":"S0-04: SVG canvas component with viewBox, managed via ref","description":"Create the core SVG canvas component. The canvas is an SVG element managed imperatively via React refs — it is outside React's virtual DOM reconciliation. The SVG element should have a viewBox set such that 1 SVG user unit = 1mm. For a default A4 document (210mm × 297mm), viewBox='0 0 210 297'. The component should: (1) render a full-size SVG element in the canvas area, (2) set viewBox based on document dimensions, (3) provide ref-based access for imperative DOM manipulation, (4) render a white artboard rectangle as the document background. Include tests verifying viewBox setup and coordinate mapping.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:28:44Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:28:44Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-6tx","title":"S1-01: Coordinate system — viewBox mapping and screen↔document coordinate conversion","description":"Implement the coordinate system utilities. Create functions to convert between screen (pixel) coordinates and document (mm) coordinates, accounting for the current viewBox, zoom level, and SVG element position on screen. Key functions: (1) screenToDoc(screenX, screenY) → {x, y} in mm, (2) docToScreen(docX, docY) → {x, y} in pixels. These use SVG's getScreenCTM() or equivalent. The mapping is: 1 SVG user unit = 1mm. At 100% zoom, 1mm ≈ 3.78 screen pixels (96 DPI). Include comprehensive tests with known coordinate pairs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:28:51Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:28:51Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-7gs","title":"S1-02: Zoom — scroll wheel with coordinate-stable zoom towards cursor","description":"Implement zoom via scroll wheel. Zoom should be coordinate-stable: the document point under the cursor stays under the cursor as zoom changes. Zoom works by modifying the SVG viewBox. Scroll up = zoom in, scroll down = zoom out. Zoom levels should be clamped to a reasonable range (e.g., 10% to 6400%). Store current zoom level in state for display in the status bar. The zoom math: on scroll at screen point P, convert P to document coords, adjust viewBox width/height by zoom factor, then reposition viewBox so that P maps back to the same document point. Include tests for zoom math.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:02Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:02Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-kct","title":"S1-05: Artboard setup — document dimensions dialog, updates viewBox","description":"Implement the artboard/document setup. Create a dialog or panel where the user can set document dimensions (width × height) in mm or cm. Provide presets for common sizes (A4: 210×297mm, A3: 297×420mm, Letter: 215.9×279.4mm, custom). Orientation toggle (portrait/landscape) swaps width and height. When dimensions change, update the SVG viewBox and root element width/height attributes. Store document metadata (dimensions, units) in the SVG \u003cmetadata\u003e element or namespaced attributes. Default to A4 portrait on new document.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:22Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:22Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-n3w","title":"S2-01: Document model — thin SVG DOM wrapper with element add/remove/modify","description":"Create the document model: a thin wrapper around the SVG DOM that provides a clean API for manipulating the document. The wrapper should support: (1) addElement(parentId, element) — add an SVG element to a parent group/layer, (2) removeElement(id) — remove an element by ID, (3) modifyAttribute(id, attr, value) — change an attribute on an element, (4) getElement(id) — retrieve an element, (5) serialize() — export the full SVG DOM as an SVG string. All elements get unique IDs assigned on creation. The document model is the single source of truth — all mutations go through it. Layer support: top-level \u003cg\u003e elements with data-layer-name attributes represent layers. Include tests for all CRUD operations and serialization.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:31Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:31Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-ysp","title":"S2-02: Command history — base command interface (execute/undo) and history stack","description":"Implement the command pattern for undo/redo. Define a Command interface with: execute(), undo(), and a human-readable description. Create the CommandHistory class that maintains: (1) an undo stack of executed commands, (2) a redo stack cleared on new command execution. Methods: execute(cmd) — runs cmd.execute() and pushes to undo stack, undo() — pops from undo stack, calls cmd.undo(), pushes to redo stack, redo() — pops from redo stack, calls cmd.execute(), pushes to undo stack, canUndo/canRedo getters. Implement initial concrete commands: AddElementCommand, RemoveElementCommand, ModifyAttributeCommand — each wrapping the corresponding document model operation and its inverse. Include thorough tests for execute/undo/redo sequences.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:42Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:42Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-vwl","title":"S2-03: Undo/redo keybindings — Ctrl+Z and Ctrl+Shift+Z wired to command history","description":"Wire up keyboard shortcuts for undo/redo. Ctrl+Z triggers undo, Ctrl+Shift+Z triggers redo. Register these as global keyboard event handlers. They should: (1) call commandHistory.undo()/redo(), (2) update the canvas to reflect the reverted/reapplied state, (3) be disabled (no-op) when canUndo/canRedo is false. Ensure the shortcuts don't conflict with text editing (when a text input is focused, defer to browser defaults). Include tests verifying the keybinding wiring.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:48Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:48Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-3d3","title":"S3-01: Tool registry — register/switch tools, active tool state, keyboard shortcuts","description":"Create the tool registry system. Each tool registers itself with: name (string), icon (React component or SVG path), keyboard shortcut (single key, e.g., 'v' for select, 'l' for line), mouse/keyboard event handlers (onMouseDown, onMouseMove, onMouseUp, onKeyDown, onClick). The registry provides: registerTool(config), setActiveTool(name), getActiveTool(), getAllTools(). Active tool state is global. When the active tool changes, event dispatch routes input events to the new tool's handlers. Keyboard shortcuts switch the active tool (only when no text input is focused). Include tests for registration, switching, and shortcut dispatch.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:56Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:29:56Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-amv","title":"S3-02: Line tool — click start, click end, creates \u003cline\u003e via AddElement command","description":"Implement the line drawing tool. Workflow: (1) select line tool (shortcut: 'l'), (2) click on canvas to set start point, (3) move mouse — a preview line follows the cursor, (4) click again to set end point and commit. The committed line is created as an SVG \u003cline\u003e element via an AddElementCommand (undoable). Line attributes: x1, y1, x2, y2 in document coordinates (mm), stroke color from current default style, stroke-width from current default. Coordinates are converted from screen to document space using screenToDoc(). Include tests for line creation and coordinate conversion.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:30:02Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:30:02Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-ogq","title":"S4-01: Selection tool — click to select, click empty to deselect, visual bounding box","description":"Implement the selection tool (shortcut: 'v'). Click on an SVG element to select it. Click on empty canvas to deselect all. Selection state is managed globally. When an element is selected, display a visual bounding box around it (a dashed rectangle overlay showing the element's bounds). The bounding box is rendered as a non-document SVG overlay (not part of the document model). Use element.getBBox() to compute bounds. Selection should work with all element types: \u003cline\u003e, \u003crect\u003e, \u003cellipse\u003e, \u003cpath\u003e, \u003ctext\u003e, \u003cg\u003e. Include tests for hit detection and selection state management.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:30:31Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:30:31Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-9gy","title":"S4-05: Move — arrow key nudge (1mm) and shift+arrow (10mm)","description":"Add keyboard-based nudge movement for selected objects. Arrow keys move selected objects by 1mm (1 SVG unit) in the corresponding direction. Shift+arrow moves by 10mm. Each nudge creates a MoveElementCommand (undoable). If no objects are selected, arrow keys do nothing. Multiple rapid nudges could optionally be coalesced into a single undo step (nice to have, not required for MVP). Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:04Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:04Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-7kg","title":"S7-06: Copy / paste / duplicate — Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V, Ctrl+D","description":"Implement clipboard operations. Copy (Ctrl+C): serialize selected elements as SVG fragments and store in an internal clipboard (not system clipboard for MVP — system clipboard integration is complex). Cut (Ctrl+X): copy then delete (undoable). Paste (Ctrl+V): deserialize clipboard SVG fragments, create new elements offset slightly from originals (+5mm, +5mm), add to active layer via AddElementCommands. Duplicate (Ctrl+D): copy + paste in one step, offset from original. All operations are undoable. Pasted elements get new unique IDs. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:33:20Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:33:20Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-acz","title":"S10-03: SVG export — serialize document model to SVG, save via Tauri file dialog","description":"Implement SVG export. Serialize the entire document model to a clean SVG string using the document model's serialize() method. Open a native file save dialog via Tauri's dialog API (tauri-plugin-dialog). Write the SVG string to the chosen file path via Tauri's fs API (tauri-plugin-fs). The exported SVG should be well-formed, with proper XML declaration and SVG namespace. Strip editor-only metadata/overlays (selection boxes, handles) from the export. Default filename suggestion based on document name. For dev mode without Tauri, fall back to a browser download. Include tests for serialization output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:35:15Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:35:15Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-cc5","title":"S7-05: Group / ungroup — group selected objects into \u003cg\u003e, ungroup","description":"Implement grouping and ungrouping. Group (Ctrl+G): take all selected elements, wrap them in a new \u003cg\u003e element within the active layer. The group becomes the new selection. A group behaves as a single object for selection and transforms. Ungroup (Ctrl+Shift+G): if a \u003cg\u003e element is selected, move its children to the group's parent and remove the empty \u003cg\u003e. Preserve the visual position and transforms of children when ungrouping. Both operations are undoable. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:33:14Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:33:14Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-l8d","title":"S7-01: Layers panel UI — list of layers, add/delete layer","description":"Create the layers panel component (left side of app, collapsible). It displays a list of layers in the document. Each layer corresponds to a top-level \u003cg\u003e element with a data-layer-name attribute. The panel shows: layer name (editable on double-click), a colored indicator. Actions: 'Add layer' button creates a new \u003cg\u003e element via AddElementCommand. 'Delete layer' button removes the selected layer and all its children (with confirmation if non-empty). The default document starts with one layer named 'Layer 1'. Clicking a layer makes it the active layer. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:32:43Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:32:43Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-rdv","title":"S6-01: Properties panel shell — context-sensitive, shows for selected object","description":"Create the properties panel component (right side of app, collapsible). The panel is context-sensitive: it shows different content based on what is selected. When nothing is selected, show document properties (artboard dimensions). When an element is selected, show transform properties (X, Y, W, H, rotation) and style properties (stroke, fill). When text is selected, additionally show font properties. The panel should have sections with headers that can be collapsed. Use Tailwind CSS, small dense controls per design principles. This is the shell — specific property inputs are added in subsequent issues.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:32:04Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:32:04Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-b47","title":"S6-03: Color picker — preset palette (16-20 colors) + hex input","description":"Create a reusable color picker component. It should include: (1) A preset palette of 16-20 commonly useful colors arranged in a grid (black, white, grays, primary colors, common diagram colors). (2) A hex input field where the user can type a hex color code (e.g., '#ff0000' or 'ff0000'). (3) RGB input fields (R, G, B as 0-255). The color picker is used in the properties panel for both fill and stroke color. It appears as a small color swatch that expands into a popover/dropdown when clicked. Include a 'none' option for transparent/no fill. Include tests for hex parsing and color conversion.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:32:16Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:32:16Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-2z8","title":"S5-01: Selection bounding box with scale handles (8 points)","description":"Enhance the selection bounding box to include 8 interactive scale handles: 4 corners and 4 edge midpoints. Handles are small squares (e.g., 6×6 pixels, rendered in screen space so they stay the same size regardless of zoom). The handles are part of the non-document overlay layer. Each handle has a distinct cursor (nwse-resize, nesw-resize, ew-resize, ns-resize as appropriate). Handles are positioned using the selected element(s) bounding box. For multi-selection, handles surround the union bounding box. Include tests for handle positioning.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:25Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:25Z"}

new_value: Implemented 8 scale handles on selection bounding box. Handles are positioned at corners and edge midpoints, sized at 6px screen-space, with correct resize cursors. Handles survive zoom changes. 10 new tests added, all 76 tests passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-asi","title":"S8-01: Pen tool — click to place anchor points connected by straight segments","description":"Implement the pen tool (shortcut: 'p') for creating paths. Initial mode: straight line segments. Workflow: (1) Click to place the first anchor point. (2) Each subsequent click places a new anchor point connected by a straight line to the previous one. (3) A preview line shows from the last anchor to the cursor. (4) The path is built incrementally as an SVG \u003cpath\u003e element using M (moveto) and L (lineto) commands. The path is added to the document via AddElementCommand on completion (see S8-03 and S8-04 for closing/finishing). Anchor points are displayed as small squares during drawing. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:33:35Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:33:35Z"}

new_value: Implemented pen tool (shortcut P). Click to place anchor points connected by straight segments with M/L path commands. Shows preview path, rubber-band line, and anchor squares during drawing. Enter to finish, Escape to cancel. Fully undoable. 14 new tests, all 90 tests passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-nyo","title":"S9-01: Point text tool — click to place, enter edit mode, type to create \u003ctext\u003e","description":"Implement the point text tool (shortcut: 't'). Workflow: (1) Click on the canvas to place a text insertion point. (2) A text cursor (blinking caret) appears at the clicked position. (3) The user types — characters are rendered as an SVG \u003ctext\u003e element at that position. (4) Clicking elsewhere or pressing Escape exits text editing mode and commits the text element via AddElementCommand. The text element uses the current default font family, size, and color. Position the \u003ctext\u003e element at the click point's document coordinates. The x,y attributes set the text baseline position. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:24Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:24Z"}

new_value: Implemented text tool (shortcut T). Click to place, type to create SVG text element with blinking caret. Enter/Escape commits text. Keyboard capture prevents tool shortcuts during typing. Fully undoable. 14 new tests, all 104 tests passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-c1p","title":"S8-04: Pen tool — finish open path (Enter or double-click)","description":"Extend the pen tool: the user can finish an open path (without closing it) by pressing Enter or double-clicking. On Enter or double-click, the current path is committed to the document as-is via AddElementCommand (no Z command appended). The pen tool resets and is ready for a new path. Pressing Escape while drawing cancels the current path and discards it. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:00Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:00Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-b2i","title":"S8-03: Pen tool — close path by clicking first anchor","description":"Extend the pen tool: when the user clicks on the first anchor point of the current path, the path is closed. This appends a Z command to the SVG path data, connecting the last point back to the first. The closed path is committed to the document via AddElementCommand. If the user drags when clicking the first anchor, the closing segment becomes a Bézier curve. After closing, the pen tool resets and is ready to start a new path. Visual feedback: highlight the first anchor when the cursor is near it (snap radius ~5px). Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:33:54Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:33:54Z"}

new_value: Implemented close-path by clicking first anchor (Z command), first-anchor red highlight on hover, double-click to finish, Enter to finish open path, Escape to cancel. 6 new tests, all 110 tests passing.

Implemented close-path by clicking first anchor (Z command), first-anchor red highlight on hover, double-click to finish, Enter to finish open path, Escape to cancel. 6 new tests, all 110 tests passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-nqx","title":"S5-02: Scale — drag corner handle, shift-constrain proportions","description":"Implement scaling via dragging scale handles. Dragging a corner handle scales the selected object(s) from the opposite corner as the anchor point. The scale factor is computed from the ratio of new size to original size. Holding Shift constrains proportions (aspect ratio preserved). Edge midpoint handles scale in one axis only. During drag, show a real-time preview of the scaled object(s). On mouse release, create a ScaleCommand (undoable) that modifies the element's transform attribute or geometry attributes. Include tests for scale math and proportion constraint.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:32Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:32Z"}

new_value: Implemented scale-drag via handles. Corner handles scale both axes, edge handles scale one axis. Shift constrains proportions. Supports rect, ellipse, circle, line, text elements. Fully undoable via reset-then-execute pattern. All 110 tests passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-7wf","title":"S11-01: Keyboard shortcut map — all tools and actions have shortcuts, displayed in tooltips","description":"Audit and finalize the complete keyboard shortcut map for all tools and actions. Ensure every tool has a single-key shortcut: V (select), A (direct select), L (line), R (rectangle), E (ellipse), P (pen), T (text), X (eraser). Ensure all actions have shortcuts: Ctrl+Z (undo), Ctrl+Shift+Z (redo), Ctrl+G (group), Ctrl+Shift+G (ungroup), Ctrl+C/X/V/D (clipboard), Delete/Backspace (delete), Ctrl+]/[ and Ctrl+Shift+]/[ (arrange), arrows (nudge). Display shortcuts in toolbar button tooltips and in a help dialog (? key). No conflicts between shortcuts. Include a test that verifies no duplicate shortcut bindings.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:35:41Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:35:41Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-7zx","title":"S11-02: Cursor icons — tool-appropriate cursors for each tool","description":"Implement context-appropriate cursor icons for each tool. Crosshair cursor for drawing tools (line, rectangle, ellipse, pen). Default/pointer cursor for selection tool. Move cursor when hovering over a selected element in selection mode. Grab/grabbing cursor during pan. Text cursor (I-beam) for text tool. Eraser cursor for eraser tool. Resize cursors for scale handles (nwse-resize, nesw-resize, ew-resize, ns-resize). Rotate cursor for rotation handle. Use CSS cursor property on the canvas element, switching based on active tool and hover state. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:35:49Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:35:49Z"}

new_value: Added tool cursors (crosshair for drawing, text for text, not-allowed for eraser, default for select), cursor property on ToolConfig, Canvas subscribes to tool changes for cursor updates, tooltips show shortcuts, no-duplicate-shortcut test added. All 111 tests passing.

Added tool cursors (crosshair for drawing, text for text, not-allowed for eraser, default for select), cursor property on ToolConfig, Canvas subscribes to tool changes for cursor updates, tooltips show shortcuts, no-duplicate-shortcut test added. All 111 tests passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-b84","title":"S5-04: Rotation handle on selection bounding box","description":"Add a rotation handle to the selection bounding box. The handle is positioned above the top-center of the bounding box, connected by a short line. It uses a rotate cursor icon. The handle is part of the non-document overlay, rendered in screen space (constant size regardless of zoom). When the rotation handle is visible, the 8 scale handles remain visible too. The rotation handle only appears when exactly one element or group is selected (multi-select rotation is complex — defer to future). Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:43Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:43Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-nto","title":"S9-03: Font family dropdown — enumerate system fonts or hardcoded safe list","description":"Add a font family selector to the properties panel for text elements. For MVP, use a hardcoded list of safe/common fonts that are widely available: sans-serif (Arial/Helvetica), serif (Times New Roman/Georgia), monospace (Courier New/Consolas), and a few others (Verdana, Trebuchet MS, etc.). Render the dropdown with each font name displayed in that font for preview. Selecting a font updates the selected text element's font-family attribute via ModifyAttributeCommand (undoable). System font enumeration via Tauri is deferred to research issue R-02. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:39Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:39Z"}

Rotation handle added (circle above top-center, connected by line, grab cursor, single-selection only). Font family dropdown with 11 safe fonts added to properties panel for text elements. 3 new tests, all 114 passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-5b5","title":"S5-03: Scale — numeric input for exact dimensions/factor","description":"Add numeric inputs in the properties panel for precise scaling. When object(s) are selected, show Width and Height fields (in mm). Editing width or height scales the object to the target dimension. Add a lock aspect ratio toggle (chain icon) — when locked, changing one dimension proportionally changes the other. Also support typing a scale factor (e.g., '2x' or '50%'). Each change creates a ScaleCommand (undoable). Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:37Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:37Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-l9g","title":"S9-04: Font size and letter-spacing inputs in properties panel","description":"Add font-size and letter-spacing numeric inputs to the properties panel for text elements. Font size: numeric input in points (pt) or mm. Common sizes as presets (8, 10, 12, 14, 18, 24, 36, 48, 72). Changing font size updates the selected text element's font-size attribute via ModifyAttributeCommand. Letter spacing: numeric input in mm. Updates letter-spacing attribute. Both inputs show current values when a text element is selected. When multiple text elements are selected with differing values, show a placeholder. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:45Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:45Z"}

new_value: Added lock aspect ratio toggle for Size inputs (rect W/H, ellipse RX/RY). Added letter-spacing (Lsp) input to text Font section. All 114 tests passing.
Added lock aspect ratio toggle for Size inputs (rect W/H, ellipse RX/RY). Added letter-spacing (Lsp) input to text Font section. All 114 tests passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-r84","title":"S5-05: Rotate — drag rotation handle, shift-constrain to 15° increments","description":"Implement rotation via dragging the rotation handle. Rotation is around the center of the selected element's bounding box. The rotation angle is computed from the angle between the vector from center to the initial mouse position and the vector from center to the current mouse position. Holding Shift constrains rotation to 15° increments (snaps to nearest 15°). During drag, show a real-time preview. On mouse release, create a RotateCommand (undoable) that sets or modifies the element's transform='rotate(angle, cx, cy)' attribute. Include tests for rotation math and snapping.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:48Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:48Z"}

new_value: Rotation via dragging rotation handle works. Angle computed from atan2 vectors relative to bbox center. Shift constrains to 15-degree increments. Sets transform=rotate(angle, cx, cy). Fully undoable. All 114 tests passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-v90","title":"S5-06: Rotate — numeric input for exact angle","description":"Add a rotation angle input field in the properties panel. When an element is selected, show the current rotation angle in degrees. The user can type an exact angle (e.g., '45') and the element rotates to that absolute angle around its bounding box center. Each change creates a RotateCommand (undoable). If the element already has a rotation transform, the displayed angle reflects the current rotation. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:53Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:31:53Z"}

new_value: Added rotation angle input (Rot) to Transform section in properties panel. Shows current rotation extracted from transform attribute, allows typing exact angle. Undoable. All 114 tests passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-70v","title":"S8-02: Pen tool — drag on place to create cubic Bézier control handles","description":"Extend the pen tool: when the user clicks and drags (instead of just clicking), the drag creates cubic Bézier control handles for that anchor point. The drag direction and distance define the outgoing control handle; the incoming control handle is mirrored (smooth point). The path segment from the previous anchor to this one becomes a cubic Bézier curve (C command in SVG path data). During drag, display control handles as small circles connected to the anchor by thin lines. The preview segment updates in real-time to show the resulting curve. Include tests for Bézier path data generation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:33:47Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:33:47Z"}

new_value: Pen tool extended with cubic Bezier support. Click-drag creates control handles (outgoing + mirrored incoming). Path uses C command for curved segments, L for straight. Handle visuals (circles + lines) shown during drag. buildPathD exported for testing. All 109 tests passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-3rv","title":"S9-02: Text editing — cursor, selection, basic keyboard navigation","description":"Implement in-place text editing. When a text element is double-clicked (or created via text tool), enter edit mode. In edit mode: (1) show a blinking cursor at the insertion point, (2) left/right arrow keys move the cursor, (3) Home/End jump to start/end, (4) click within the text to reposition cursor, (5) Shift+arrow/click to select a range of text (highlighted), (6) Backspace/Delete to remove characters, (7) typing inserts at cursor position. All edits modify the \u003ctext\u003e element's text content. On exit from edit mode, create a ModifyAttributeCommand capturing the before/after text content. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:31Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:31Z"}

new_value: Full text editing with cursor positioning: ArrowLeft/Right navigation, Home/End, insert at cursor, Backspace/Delete at cursor, Shift+arrow selection, Ctrl+A select all, typing replaces selection. Selection visual with blue highlight rect. 16 tests. All 111 passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-03p","title":"S8-05: Direct selection — click anchor points on paths, drag to move","description":"Implement the direct selection tool (shortcut: 'a'). When active and the user clicks on a path element, the path's individual anchor points are displayed as small squares. Clicking an anchor point selects it (highlighted). Dragging a selected anchor point moves it, updating the corresponding coordinates in the SVG path data. The path re-renders in real-time during drag. On mouse release, a ModifyAttributeCommand is created (undoable) that records the path data change. Only anchor points of \u003cpath\u003e elements are directly selectable — for \u003cline\u003e, \u003crect\u003e, \u003cellipse\u003e, the selection tool handles these. Include tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:08Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:08Z"}

new_value: Direct selection tool (shortcut A) implemented. Click on path shows anchor points as interactive squares. Click to select an anchor, drag to move it, updating path d attribute in real-time. Undoable via ModifyAttributeCommand. parsePathAnchors/updatePathAnchor utilities for M/L/C path parsing. 10 new tests, all 121 passing.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-ad3","title":"S8-06: Direct selection — display and drag control handles","description":"Extend the direct selection tool: when an anchor point on a Bézier path is selected, display its control handles (small circles connected to the anchor by thin lines). Dragging a control handle changes the curve shape by modifying the control point coordinates in the SVG path data. For smooth anchor points, moving one control handle mirrors the other. Real-time preview during drag. On mouse release, create a ModifyAttributeCommand (undoable). Include tests for control handle display and path data modification.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:14Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:34:14Z"}

new_value: Direct selection extended: when anchor is selected, Bezier control handles (circles + lines) are displayed. Clicking/dragging handles modifies cp1/cp2 in path d attribute. parsePathWithHandles and updatePathControlPoint utilities added. 6 new tests, all 127 passing.

Research complete: Tauri v2 works on WSL2+WSLg with WebKitGTK. Requires proper -dev deps via native package manager. GPU permissions may need setup. Windows cross-compilation not recommended — use CI/CD (GitHub Actions) instead. WSLg sufficient for Linux dev/testing.

Research complete: No official Tauri font plugin. Best approach: Rust Tauri command using font-kit crate (SystemSource API) for cross-platform font enumeration (DirectWrite/CoreText/Fontconfig). Returns structured JSON. Alternative: fc-list via shell (Linux-only). Current hardcoded 11-font list is fine for MVP.

Research complete: Current element support (rect, ellipse, circle, line, path, text, g) covers core needs. Strategy: normalize CSS styles to presentation attributes on edit, preserve unsupported elements with data-unsupported=true for round-trip fidelity, fix transform preservation on import. use/defs: store as-is, add break-apart command later.

old_value: {"id":"vectorfeld-486","title":"Rotation breaks bounding box and movement","description":"CRITICAL: After rotating an element, the bounding box stays axis-aligned. After moving a rotated element, the bounding box fully detaches. Root cause: rotation center (cx,cy) baked into SVG transform rotate(angle,cx,cy) becomes stale when moveElement() changes x/y. Also getBBox() ignores transforms so overlay is always axis-aligned. Fix: adopt translate(tx,ty) rotate(angle) transform strategy. Files: selectTool.ts:180-197,413, selection.ts:139","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T13:54:08Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T13:54:08Z"}

{"id":"vectorfeld-718","title":"Properties panel shows 15+ decimal places","description":"Position shows 48.85975646972656, size shows 90.54878234863281. Root cause: getAttr() returns raw attribute strings with zero formatting. StatusBar already uses .toFixed(1). Fix: format display values to 2 decimal places. File: PropertiesPanel.tsx:22-24","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T13:54:12Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-01T13:54:12Z"}
